refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and the operational context (agreed network conditions), enabling targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and compare against customer-defined limits.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to relevant modules.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.
DP2: Use dedicated high-reliability network link with QoS prioritization.
DP3: Integrate real-time packet loss monitoring and dynamic adjustment algorithms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol and teleoperation control logic.
Dependency on network link quality and safety alert timing.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates communication quality management, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of increased latency due to retransmissions affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
Safety criticality requires robust fallback mechanisms.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding fallback DP for degraded network conditions to enhance safety.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation control, communication management, and real-time data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection supporting data transmission with adaptive protocol.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends communication quality metrics and status to NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands from NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts if communication quality degrades below acceptable thresholds.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of communication protocol logic and monitoring; low diffusion as functionality is centralized.
Contains overall system coordination but less focused on communication specifics.
Limited containment; communication logic not primarily in vehicle module.
Receives alerts but does not contain communication protocol.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing communication quality management within Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and supports clear ownership.
containment_risks: Potential overload of Teleoperation module with communication and control responsibilities.
Interface complexity with Safety Services for alerting may increase coupling.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate splitting monitoring and alerting functions into Safety Services if diffusion risk increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with clear mechanisms to control packet loss.

diffusion_risks: Some risk of diffusion if communication monitoring overlaps with Safety Services alerting.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor Teleoperation module, supporting architectural clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring packet loss metrics via Teleoperation interfaces and network link testing.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of communication quality responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Consider fallback DP for degraded network conditions.
Monitor diffusion risk between Teleoperation and Safety Services for alerting.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition sufficiently refines the FR, selects a viable DP, and assigns a clear IM boundary with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to relevant modules.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can handle communication protocol complexity.
open_risks: Diffusion risk between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
evidence_needed: Performance data on packet loss under adaptive protocol.
Interface load and alerting effectiveness between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
